LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRIAN SCHMITT, KRISTA STRYKER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT DEFENDANT MAY REOPEN PLAINTIFFS' DEPOSITIONS WITH RESPECT TO ANY NEW ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT UNDULY DELAYED IN PRESENTING THEIR MOTION. DEFENDANT MAY CHALLENGE THE BASIS FOR PLAINTIFFS' NEWLY ASSERTED CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES BY A MOTION TO STRIKE OR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND ADD THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TO BE FILED WITHIN 10 DAYS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: RUTH EKHAUS, CSR#12219, REKHAUS@SFTC.ORG

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RUTH MAGINNIS'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFFS AMENDED SUBPOENAS TO KASIER HOSPITAL - SAN FRANCISCO AND ZUCKERBERG SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL. THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 9, 2019, AT 2 PM BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM HIGHMAN. THE ONLY RELEVANCE PLAINTIFF ASSERTS FOR THE DISCOVERY IS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANT HAS MOVED TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVES. THE HEARING ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS ON JULY 25. IF THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED, THEN THIS MOTION BECOMES MOOT. THERE IS NO NEED FOR PLAINTIFF TO HAVE THE DISCOVERY BEFORE AUGUST. IF THE DISCOVERY WERE ALLOWED BEFORE THE COURT RULES ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE, AND THE COURT THEN GRANTED THE MOTION TO STRIKE, THEN ALLOWING THE DISCOVERY WOULD HAVE BEEN AN UNWARRANTED INTRUSION INTO DEFENDANT'S PRIVACY. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFFS BRIAN SCHMITT AND KRISTA STRYKER'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES SPECIFIED IN THE PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT PLAINTIFF BRIAN SCHMITT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM DOUGLAS ROBBINS IS ADOPTED IN FULL. THE AMENDMENT TO THE COMPLAINT TO ADD A PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATION ABOUT OPERATING THE VEHICLE WHILE INTOXICATED PUTS DEFENDANT RUTH MAGINNIS' PRESCRIPTION DRUG USE AT ISSUE. THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IN FORM INTERROGATORY 2.13 (C)-(G) IS DIRECTLY RELEVANT SUCH THAT IT OUTWEIGHS MAGGINIS' CONSTITUTIONAL PRIVACY RIGHT, ESPECIALLY GIVEN THAT MAGGINIS HAS ALREADY REVEALED THE THREE KINDS OF PRESCRIPTION MEDICINES SHE TAKES. STAYED UNTIL JULY 25, 2019 TO ENABLE MOTION TO STRIKE.PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Amended Subpoenas To Kasier Hospital - San Francisco And Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Continued To Aug-09-19 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RUTH MAGINNIS's Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Amended Subpoenas To Kasier Hospital - San Francisco And Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital.

The hearing on this motion is continued to August 9, 2019, at 2 pm before Judge Pro Tem Highman. The only relevance plaintiff asserts for the discovery is for punitive damages. Defendant has moved to strike the punitives. The hearing on the motion to strike is on July 25. If the motion to strike is granted, then this motion becomes moot. There is no need for plaintiff to have the discovery before August. If the discovery were allowed before the court rules on the motion to strike, and the court then granted the motion to strike, then allowing the discovery would have been an unwarranted intrusion into defendant's privacy.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To The Form Interrogatories Specified In The Plaintiffs Separate Statement
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ugo O. Asobie, esq. (213-385-9991) for Plaintiffs via court call.
Morgan O. Durham, esq. (707-427-3998) of McNamara, Ney...Ambacher LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs BRIAN SCHMITT and KRISTA STRYKER's Motion To Compel Further Responses To The Form Interrogatories Specified In The Plaintiff's Separate Statement

Plaintiff Brian Schmitt's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is GRANTED. Sanctions are DENIED. The Report of Judge Pro Tem Douglas Robbins is adopted in full.

The amendment to the complaint to add a punitive damages allegation about operating the vehicle while intoxicated puts Defendant Ruth Maginnis' prescription drug use at issue. The information sought in Form Interrogatory 2.13 (c)-(g) is directly relevant such that it outweighs Magginis' constitutional privacy right, especially given that Magginis has already revealed the three kinds of prescription medicines she takes. Stayed until July 25, 2019 to enable motion to strike.

Plaintiff's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ugo Asobie, Esq. 213-385-9991 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Morgan Durham, Esq. 707-427-3998 of McNamara, Ney, Beatty, Slattery, Borges & Ambacher for Defendant Ruth Maginnis.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRIAN SCHMITT, KRISTA STRYKER'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Brian Schmitt and Krista Stryker's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted on the condition that Defendant may reopen Plaintiffs' depositions with respect to any new allegations. Plaintiffs have not unduly delayed in presenting their motion. Defendant may challenge the basis for Plaintiffs' newly asserted claim for punitive damages by a motion to strike or motion for summary judgment/adjudication.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and add the first amended complaint is to be filed within 10 days in compliance with CRC 3.1312. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ruth Ekhaus, CSR#12219, rekhaus@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)
